releases.author: samaraliwarsi
releases.description:
## Version 1.4.2

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 New updates

- **Side Portal on Right** - Each side portal can now be opened on the right side Obsidian side bar. 
- Minor bug fixes

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/plugins/portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safety measure. 
 >- For best results,
> 	- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
> 	- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side. 

### ✨ General feature list

- **Pin folders & tags** as tabs at the top of the file pane.
- **Stacks** – group multiple tabs into collapsible stacks.
- **Custom icons & colors** – tabs, folders, files, tag groups.
- **Six visual styles** + compact tree view + bold folder names.
- **Tag grouping & subtag support** for tag portals.
- **Multi‑select** files, folders, and tags with `Alt+Click` or `Swipe right`. Use multi-select toolbar for actions. 
- **Command palette** to add or remove portals and configure side portals. 
- **Foldable floating action buttons** for  note/ folder creation, tag grouping, collapse and sort. 
- **Custom display names** for tabs, stacks. 
- **Side Portal** - A modular, collapsible, resizable view containing, **Bookmarks**, **Recent Files**, **Context notes**, **Hidden**, **Properties** and **Journal**. 
	- **Context Notes** - an expansion to the older Folder notes feature. Context notes apply to folder as well as tags. 
	- **Recents** - Shows recent files list from across the vault. 
	- **Bookmarks** - Shows bookmarked files, folders or web links.
	- **Journal** - A viewing and marking tool for Daily Notes with a quote display feature. 
	- **Hidden** - Place to show files, tags and folders hidden from the main view. 
	- **Properties** - A viewing tool for markdown files, based on their properties. 
- **Drag & drop** reordering for portal tabs and stacks (desktop).
- **Mobile friendly** – Responsive design that works on small screens. Tested on Android (more platforms coming).
- **Export/Import settings** – Backup your tab configuration or transfer it to another vault using json files from `Settings`.

### ⚙️ Installation

#### Using BRAT (Beta Reviewers Auto-update Tester)

- Install BRAT from Community Plugins
- Open Settings, **Add Beta Plugin**
- Enter Repository URL: [[GitHub](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals)
- Click **Add Plugin**, BRAT will download and enable the latest release.

#### Manual download

- Download release package.
- Copy the files named `main.js`, `styles.css`, `manifest.json` and place in `.obsidian/plugins/portals/`

### 📝 Notes

- See the [[README](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme) and [[Portals Guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md) for full details and screenshots.

- **Header Tooltips** - All folders and tags now display tooltips. The earlier setting for `Show file tooltips` has changed to `Show tree item tooltips`. 	- **Context Notes** - an expansion to the older Folder notes feature. Context notes apply to folder as well as tags. 
	- **Recents** - Shows recent files list from across the vault.

## Version 1.3.9

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 New updates

- **Trash** - removed trash polling method to go for simpler obsidian native file listeners and debounce refresh. This improves overall plugin performance and startup times when trash is active. 
- **File explorer** Fixed an issue with DOM element map that could potentially lead to memory leaks in edge cases. 
- **Journal** - Fixed an issue where certain components of journal would linger even if journal was closed or inactive. Added additional checks to ensure journal stays self-contained. 
- **Icon picker** - Fixed an issue that prevents silent errors in icon picker in edge cases.
- **Tag groups** - Fixed an issue where tag groups in tag tree view could persist even after user removing the tag completely from the vault. 
- **File Renaming** - There were a few issues with file renaming method that have been fixed. Inline rename could sometimes appear under the newly added lucide icons, or under the newly implemented file-action-icon for preview toggling. Inline rename could, in edge cases, stay locked even when user has moved to other actions. Inline rename also now hides action-icons, quick-icons and extension badge while it's active. 

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/plugins/portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safety measure. 
 >- For best results,

## Version 1.3.5

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 New updates

- **Settings page** - Settings page is redesigned to show separate pages instead of a single, long scrolling page.
- **File preview** - If enabled from settings, Markdown files can display a content snippet below the filename in the file tree. 
	- Previews show file tags in folder spaces, and folders in tag spaces, if `Show file info bar` is enabled in **Explorer settings**.
	- Toggle preview per file using the hover icon or via multi-select toolbar using the `Toggle preview` action. Preferences of per file collapse and expand are stored in user data.json.
	- If a file has no content, no preview appears.
	- Disable globally using the setting or command palette, `Portals: Toggle file previews in explorer`. Users can add it to hotkey using Obsidian settings.
- **Sections** - Organise folders or tags into collapsible section breaks. Enable sections via settings, or using command palette.
	- Use the new **Sections** floating button (appears near the sort button, if sections are enabled) to define section rules:
	    - **Properties** – group by a frontmatter property. In this option, use `Right‑click` to search available properties.
	    - **Extensions** – group by file extensions.
	    - Create new properties to use for grouping with **Sections**.
	- Each section respects the current sort order. Use hover icons on section headers to reposition sections up or down.
- **Tooltips** - Markdown files can now show the word count, last modified and file name (if its getting truncated by the side bar size). This is user setting based, can be toggled on from `Show file tooltips` in **Explorer settings**
- **Tabs** - Quick switcher is a new addition. Portal tabs can now have a quick tab number. This is used to allot it to specific command palette slots which can be called from keyboard shortcuts. Eg. 
	- `Right-click` a tab to assign a quick tab number using the Quick switch tabs modal. 
	- Use command palette `Open portal` command to call for 1 and the view switches over. 
	- Assign `Open portal 1` to any keyboard combo using Obsidian native Hotkeys settings, eg. `Alt+1` to call it. 
	- 10 tabs can be assigned number using the context menu and Quick tab modal. 
	- There is an option to display a small numeric badge next to the tab, it can be turned on in the **Portal settings** page, `Show quick tab badge`. 
- **Icons** - Lucide icon pack is now a part of the portals plugin. The following updates have been introduced, 
	- New icon picker modal features both lucide and phosphor tabs. User defined custom icons can use both together. 
	- A new setting to decide system icons. Users can switch using **Utilities page** of settings, `Plugin icon library`, this only affects the hardcoded defaults like icons for default tabs, side portals, floating buttons etc. 
	- New way of user preference storage. User preference strings now store `Library:IconName` instead of icon name. A migration code inside should migrate all the data to new method seamlessly. If any icons are left, they will fallback to `question` or `help-circle` icons depending on `Plugin icon library` setting. They can be reassigned easily. 
	- Phosphor remains the native and since its not SVG based, performance using phosphor will be far better because the base string is encoded into the plugin. Lucide is called from internal Obsidian API. Both icon sets work completely offline and do not require connecting to the internet at all. 
- **Modals Redesign** - Overall modal retouch has been done for performance and visual improvements, outside the visual improvements on almost all modals, the functional improvements are in these modals, 
	- **Color Modal** - Includes color palette swatches for quick addition. Default swatches can be customised by user, customised swatch is saved in user data. 
	- **Icon Modal** - Redesigned to include **Lucide** and **Favorites page**. Lucide icons are svgs, so the loading mechanisms are different. Favorites tab is enabled if user adds favorites, strings of which are saved to user data for persistence. 
	- General modal redesign for other modals like Add Portal, Remove portal, Side portal Folder select, Rename, Confirm, Search Popover, Frontmatter modal, Quick tab switch modal and more.
- **Side Portal with commands** - All side portals can now be toggled using commands. If they're not enabled or in view, commands will enable them. Use command palette to search for a side portal and the view will switch to it. 
- **Properties** - The properties side portal now has sorting options. Sorting menu can be accessed by clicking on the filtered number badge (requires filtered number badge enabled in settings). 
- **Journal** - Journal side portal has been updated with some new features, 
	- Added a new tab to display only quotes from Marked daily notes. Daily notes can be marked using right click. 
	- Users can now choose which tab of the journal they want as default using `Journal default quote tab` in **Side Portal page** of settings.
	- Also added new tooltips in journal. 
- **Auto backup settings** - a new addition to save data.json files has been added,
	- Saves a data backup with obsidian reloads. 
	- Saves by default at vault root, can be configured to user defined folder
	- Saves 3 copies named by system date and time. 
	- Test feature - per device sync. A new cloud icon is added next to this setting. For those who switch devices quickly, this feature has a potential to cause conflicts as it saves 100ms after full obsidian reload. Using the cloud icon, users can disable the creation process on certain devices. The setting being on is still synced, only the creation process stops. This should help prevent multiple file creations with obsidian reloads on multiple devices. The files created by one device would sync anyway since they're inside the vault. 
- **More Commands** - Added new commands for `Stack all unstacked portal tabs`, `Switch to vault root tab` (if enabled), `Switch to previous portal`. A total list of all commands available is added into [[Portals_Guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/releases/new)]() and can be accessed by simply writing `Portals into the command palette`. Users can set their desired commands to hotkeys for quick access.
- **Deep CSS updates** - The styling has been refactored and cleaned to improve visual consistency and ability to work with and override theme related quirks. 
- **Bug fixes**
	- Fixed an issue with clicking to edit inline renaming. 
	- Fixed an issue with icon picker modal that limited the number of icons displayed.
	- Other minor bug fixes.

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/plugins/portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safety measure. 
 >- For best results,
> 	- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
> 	- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side. 

### ✨ General feature list
₹
- **Pin folders & tags** as tabs at the top of the file pane.
- **Stacks** – group multiple tabs into collapsible stacks.
- **Custom icons & colors** – tabs, folders, files, tag groups.
- **Six visual styles** + compact tree view + bold folder names.
- **Tag grouping & subtag support** for tag portals.
- **Multi‑select** files, folders, and tags with `Alt+Click` or `Swipe right`. Use multi-select toolbar for actions. 
- **Command palette** to add or remove portals and configure side portals. 
- **Foldable floating action buttons** for  note/ folder creation, tag grouping, collapse and sort. 
- **Custom display names** for tabs, stacks. 
- **Side Portal** - A modular, collapsible, resizable view containing, **Bookmarks**, **Recent Files**, **Context notes**, **Hidden**, **Properties** and **Journal**. 
	- **Context Notes** - an expansion to the older Folder notes feature. Context notes apply to folder as well as tags. 
	- **Recents** - Shows recent files list from across the vault. 
	- **Bookmarks** - Shows bookmarked files, folders or web links.
	- **Journal** - A viewing and marking tool for Daily Notes with a quote display feature. 
	- **Hidden** - Place to show files, tags and folders hidden from the main view. 
	- **Properties** - A viewing tool for markdown files, based on their properties. 
- **Drag & drop** reordering for portal tabs and stacks (desktop).
- **Mobile friendly** – Responsive design that works on small screens. Tested on Android (more platforms coming).
- **Export/Import settings** – Backup your tab configuration or transfer it to another vault using json files from `Settings`.

### ⚙️ Installation

#### Using BRAT (Beta Reviewers Auto-update Tester)

- Install BRAT from Community Plugins
- Open Settings, **Add Beta Plugin**
- Enter Repository URL: [[GitHub](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals)
- Click **Add Plugin**, BRAT will download and enable the latest release.

#### Manual download

- Download release package.
- Copy the files named `main.js`, `styles.css`, `manifest.json` and place in `.obsidian/plugins/portals/`

### 📝 Notes

- See the [[README](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme) and [[Portals Guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md) for full details and screenshots.

## Version 1.3.3

### 🆕 Updates

- **Listeners** - The following listeners are now enabled on all places of the plugin that carry a file display, i.e. folder/ tag tree, side portal lists, context note display, context note links and journal date cards. 
	- `Middle-click` with mouse to open file in a new tab. 
	- `Cmd/Ctrl+click` to open file in new tab.
	- `Shift+click` to open file in a vertical split window. 
- **New settings** - Two new settings to choose icon position on tabs and stacks have been added. This setting is only relevant if the tab/ stack names are not hidden. Also works on side portals. 
- **Properties side portal** - Fixed an issue where properties side portal would not refresh immediately after frontmatter changes. Properties side portal now renders immediately upon frontmatter changes, except if the **Frontmatter editing** modal is open. This allows users to edit multiple files without losing what they were editing. Once closed, the side portal will refresh again. A debounce render is added to improve performance while handling bulk editing of files. 
- **Tooltip** - Fixed an issue of a ghost tooltip that would appear towards the top left sometimes. Tooltips now clear out after they're displaying, please report on GitHub if you still spot a similar issue. 
- Visual improvement on portal tabs and stacks. 
- **Setting page** - Settings page has been simplified. Add portal items brought to top and settings positions altered for better arrangement. 
- **Context notes** - New context menu option for files (only in folder spaces). `New folder with context` uses a file it was triggered from, creates a folder with the same name and inherits the files custom color and custom icon. The file becomes context note for that folder. 

> [!Note]

releases.description:
## Version 1.2.5

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 Updates
- **Frontmatter editor** - A new popup editor to add, edit the frontmatter (properties, tags) for markdown files. It works for single file as well as multi-select files. The editor supports adding, searching for existing properties, values as well as creating new ones. For more details, see the documentation in [[Portals_guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md).
- **Properties side panel** - Properties side panel now supports context menu for files, custom colors and multi-select. A new multi-select tool bar is added to properties panel to help bulk actions, currently available options are front matter editor and reset colors. 
- **Custom Folder and Tag reordering** - Folders and Tags can be reordered from the context menu. `Reorders sub-items` context menu opens a modal where users can drag to reposition the child folders or child tags. 
	- For folders, this applies at a vault level. The repositions made in one folder portal would apply at the root folder `/` level as well. 
	- For tag portals, subtags and group tags can be rearranged in tree using the same method. 
	- At a portal level, this can also be triggered from command palette - `Cmd/Ctrl+P` and go for `Portals: Reorder folders/tags`. 
- **Range Select** - Multi-select now supports range select. Use `Alt+Shift` to click to items, range select will add all the items between the two points in the multi-select group. Same options of multi-select toolbar are available.  
- **Add Portal Modal** - The modal now supports multi-select using `Alt+click`, allowing users to add multiple portals in one go. 
- **Page Preview** - Page preview using `Cmd/Ctrl` and hover now supports Dataview queries inside context notes side Portal
- **Context notes follow active** - The follow active setting of context notes now supports tag spaces as well. However it's function differs from that of folder portal tabs. For details, please refer to Context notes section of [[Portals_Guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md#context-notes)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md#context-notes)
- **Tab color** - The setting for tab color now extends to change the colors of Context note highlight icon, openDot for files/folders and extension badge. All the items will now show user defined Tab colors when the setting is on. 
- **Recent files** - custom file colors now apply to recent files as well and can be applied from recent files side portal. 
- **Bookmarks** - Bookmarks side portal now extends styles like minimal, shades etc to bookmark groups as well. Please note that groups must be defined using Obsidian's bookmarks, once created portals can manage them, add, remove items directly from Portals file tree. 
- **Context Menus** - New canvas create option in folder context menu now triggers inline rename just like new file or new folder creation. 
- **Side Portals** - Side Portals now support drag and reorder for custom positioning.
- **Export settings** - Export settings had a bug on mobile where they would not trigger select folder on mobile. Now, on mobiles export always sends the json file to vault root folder. For desktops, the functionality is unchanged. 
- **Remove portal modal** - The modal would close immediately after removing a portal. Now it only closes via close button, users can remove multiple portals without opening it again. 
- **Settings** - The portal lists in settings now have a persistent state where they maintain user defined open or close state for the duration of the edits. The user preference carries on for the Obsidian session until restart. 
- **Open dot** - The active path open dot now supports tag spaces, i.e. Subtags and grouptags in the same way as folder spaces. 
- Edge case bug fixes:
	- Bug fix several actions of rename, delete/ create context notes, rename, reset icons etc would reset portals view scroll position. 
	- Bug fix where render on rename could potentially trigger a freeze. 
	- Edge case bug fix where context notes tab could cause listener leaks that made files open inside side portals, even after context notes side portal was closed. 
- Add memory leak preventions to side portal where the side portals features are only on when the side portal is active. 
- Overall code cleanup and refactor plus minor CSS upgrades. 

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/plugins/portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safety measure. 
 >- For best results,
> 	- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
> 	- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side. 

### ✨ General feature list

- **Pin folders & tags** as tabs at the top of the file pane.
- **Stacks** – group multiple tabs into collapsible stacks.
- **Custom icons & colors** – tabs, folders, files, tag groups.

When enabled the context note side portal will show the context note of the parent folder of the active file. If no context note exists in the parent folder, it will look one level upwards, walking up all the way till the portal space or root. If none exist, it will fallback to showing the portal tab's context note. 	- In **Context notes** side portal, direct typed links are supported as of now. A future iteration of the same will also support Dataview rendered links.

releases.description:
## Version 1.2.0

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 New Updates
- **Tab Stacks** - Individual portal stacks can now be grouped into **Stacks**. This is consolidate the portal tab view with many folders and tags, maintaining a short horizontal scrolling tab bar. The stacks are done in accordion style, allow custom colors, icons, icon colors, custom names, no names and stack count on display based on the user preferences. 
- **Hidden items** - New feature to hide files and a side portal to show hidden items. 
	- **Hidden** is a side portal that shows a list of items (files, folders, tags) that appear in the portal view. 
	- Use the context menu to hide anything. Hiding a folder/ tag will also hide it's contents. However, for a hidden tag, it's content file can still appear on another folder. 
	- Unhide options are available inside the Hidden side portal. Side portal also shows the type of item that was originally hidden, helping categorise items incase they tags and folders share the same name. 
	- Tag groups cannot be hidden, the feature is not enabled on them as they can be turned off by `Tag groups` button. 
- **Properties** - a new side portal to browse files by properties. Note that this is a viewer, not an editor. It can help browse files by properties and their values. Please note that this only counts the markdown files. 
	- The Property filter dropdown contains a list of all available frontmatter properties in markdown files of the vault. 
	- Ability to display files with no frontmatter. 
	- The Value filter dropdown can help further narrow down to sort by specific values of properties selected. 
	- User preference to show the count of files queried and the option to show the value badge on files in the list. 
	- **Cache** - Property browser works on a cache system so it lazy-loads its data listening to actual changes in file frontmatter, file create, delete, rename etc functions.  
- **Context Notes** - The folder note feature is expanding to include tag notes and as such its title name is changing to Context Notes. Just as folder notes did, context notes of portals will show up in the side portal, which also has been renamed from folder notes to context notes. List of updates,
	- **For folders**, context notes function as they were. Existing migration code automatically converts old folder-note settings to the new Context Notes settings. Context notes for folders are still saved under the head folders. 
	- **For tags, sub tags and group tags**, context notes work with some intricacies better described in [[Portals Guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md) on GitHub. Please go through to find details and preferences on on how the new Context Notes work in Portals. 
	- **Icon click to open context notes** - Context notes (for folders and tags) can now be opened by a clicking on the icon. Turn this feature on in the settings. It only works if context notes are on and if the folder/tag has an associated context note. 
	- Context notes can now be deleted directly using `Right-click` on folders or tags. This works if context notes feature is on and the selected item has a context note associated to it. 
	- Cache size increase - The cache size for side portal context notes has been increased from 10 to 20 context notes. 
- **Custom Color** - Custom color is now also available for files. This completes initial custom color implementation. It now supports folders, files, tags, subtags, portals and stacks. Unstacked portal colors and icons can now be updated directly from the context menu. 
- **MultiSelect Updates** - Multi select option launched in 1.1.7 was for files and folders. This can be triggered by `Alt-click` on desktop & `Swipe right` on mobile. The feature now supports options to hide items, reset icons and colors of all selected items. Multi-select has also been expanded to allow usage in tag spaces. Tag groups, subtags can now be multi-selected. The actions available for multi-selected items differ based on the kind of items. 
- **Journal** - The following updates have been made to journal side portal, 
	- Quote indicator to help highlight files where quote extraction was successful or files using wrong delimiters. Its a visual feedback for what files adding to the quote system and what files are not, without opening them. The quote indicator dropdown choices can identify successful extractions, warnings, both or none based on user preference. 
	- Date parse logic improvement. A clear warning if any of the files in the settings defined `Journal folder` does not match the settings defined `Journal date format`. 
- **New commands** - `Remove portal tab` and `Configure side portal tabs`. Access commands by `Cmd/Ctrl+P` or `/` on editor. 
- **Modal CSS update** - All the modals used have been given a style update. 
- The side portal triangle collapse icon `Right-click` can now open the **Configure side portal modal**. A once per session tool tip has been added to show the availability of this feature. 
- A once per session tooltip has been added on **Collapse button** in floating buttons to show the feature of `Right-click` to fold/unfold, post which it returns to its regular tooltip of Collapse/fold. 
- Fixed an issue where changing the icon of a file for the very first time resets scroll position. 
- fixed an issue where color changes and cancelling would sometimes persist color on the element. 
- CSS improvements for general look and feel smoothing
- Bug fixes and QoL minor updates.

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/plugins/portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safety measure. 
 >- For best results,
> 	- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
> 	- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side.

Custom colors are applied based on styles for more details, see [[Portals_guide](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals/blob/main/Portals_Guide.md) on GitHub.
- **Multi-Select** - Multi-select is now enabled for files and folders. It works in both tag spaces as well as folder spaces. To multi-select use `Alt+Click` on desktop on `Swipe-right` on touch devices. It works through pop-up  toolbar options that are enabled with multi-select. The current actions available are `Delete`, `Move`, `Create folder from selected` and `Deselect`. Multi-select files and folder are highlighted by a dashed border of theme accent color. 
- **Change portal tab icon** - Users can now change portal tab icon without going into settings, directly using the `Right-click` context menu option. Any changes made here are updated in settings and vice versa. 
- **Add portals from command palette** - Users can now add portals using Obsidian's command palette. `Cmd/Ctrl+P` or `/` on desktop to open command palette, type *Portals* and select `Portals: Add portal tab` to open the same modal as settings page. 
- **Journal**
	- Added support for YYYY-MM-DD format
	- Format mismatch now shows a warning in journal. Journal requires users to set their format based on the date format in their journal folder. Since there are three date format, this warning is necessary for now, to ensure consistent performance. 
- **Folder note** - `Shift+key` update. the key combo is primarily for quick create folder notes, used to give warning earlier if folder note already existed. Now, if folder note exits, the key combo will open the folder note in the current active folder. This is different from Cmd+click which always opens the available folder note in a new tab in the editor. 
- **Icon update** - Since there are no icons in the styles `Shades` or `Minimal`, the context menu options for icon change in those are muted. This doesn't change user set preferences of icons per folder/ file/ tag group/ subtag. 
- **File extension badge** - Updated. If the setting for `Extension badge for non-markdown files` is on, the file's own extension in `.ext` will be hidden.  If the setting is off, the `.ext` extension it will be visible. 
- Delete file option in context menu and multi-select toolbar now shows up in red color to clearly distinguish from the other options.
- General CSS upgrades, minor updates to the styles `Minimal` and `Boxed` to help the incoming changes with custom colors. 
- Fixed a bug where deleting files, folders would trigger a render and refresh scroll position of the file explorer. 
- Fixed a bug where changes in settings page would change the scroll position of file explorer. 
- Minor bug fixes, redundant code clean ups. 

> [!Note]
> - Please make sure to backup your data file after a full setup of your preferences, color & icon choices. You can backup by copying the file `.obsidian/Plugins/Portals/data.json` to safe location. You can also export the same via `Settings` and later import it. Make sure to backup before any plugin updates for safe measure. 
> - Side Portal is a new feature under testing. For best results,
> 	- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
> 	- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side. 

### ✨ Features

## Version 1.1.5

### 🆕 Updates
- **New Side Portal - Journal**. The side portal **Journal** is a new way to visualise your daily notes using snippets of user selected text from their daily notes. Journal displays a list of daily notes sorted by `All items`, `This month` and `This year`. The notes displayed can be marked using right-click or long press on mobile devices. The quote snippets displayed have two categories, `Random` - showing all the marked snippets in a random order, and `On this day` - a look back to the snippets marked for all the dates similar to today in the current year, and all the dates similar to today in each year going back 10 years. The snippets are displayed using a 30 second timer and refresh automatically. Users can choose to mark their notes using a delimiter of their choice from settings. The default delimiter is set to ==text==, i.e. Obsidian default for highlights. Journal is designed to work with the core Daily Notes plugin and may not work with community Daily Notes plugins that work outside of core plugin system. Journal supports date formats of DD-MM-YYYY and MM-DD-YYYY. 
- **Custom Icons for files and folders.** Users can now assign icons to their files and folders using the CSS embedded *Phosphor* icon set. Since the iconset is embedded it works offline without needing any downloads from CDN. Icons chosen are saved in the data file. Make sure to keep a backup of your data file - can be copied from `.plugins/Portals/data.json` or exported as a `.json` file from settings. Icons are vault consistent, i.e. Icons chosen at one place will be same shown at another, be it a folder portal tab or a tag portal tab.
- **Introducing Styles** - Users can now choose to customise the look of their file tree/ tag tree using preset styles. Each style provides a different look. Currently 5 styles are launched to start with, i.e. `Default`, `Minimal`, `Boxed`, `Portals`, `Hues` and `Shades`. `Minimal` and `Shades` are both minimally dressed and do not show icons. The `Shades` styles can be combined with background color option to create color shading for folders in a portal tab. The `Portals` style can use the Tab Colors setting to show the file tree with user defined color in each Portal tab. `Hues` has it's own colors that are preset, background colors are best avoided with this theme. Style chosen also extends its look to **Recents** and **Bookmarks** in side portal. 
- **Folder Notes Cache** introduced in 1.1.4 now has a hardlimit of 10 folder notes that are being cached for display in side portal. This is to ensure the cache size doesn't grow too much. This doesn't change the functionality or folder notes but helps keeps some limits to what can be displayed in side portal. In short, the caching in side portal supports the 10 most recently updated folder notes, or 10 side portal tabs in folder notes. Caching saves the effort of constant re-rendering and helps save scroll position in side portal displayed folder notes during tab switches. 
- Setting UI for color input has been updated, it no longer displays the extra preview outside the chosen color as the chosen color itself is a preview. 
- Improved tooltips. The design and appearance on desktop tooltips now appears closer to the native obsidian tooltips, giving it a consistent design approach to rest of the UI that portals sits inside of. 
- Minor visual updates to items like floating buttons, tab bars, side tab bar. 
- Fixed an issue where inactive tab names on side portal would be hidden/ cropped. Now side portal tabs can scroll horizontally. 
- Improved render, debounce and schedule render performance for large vaults. 
- Improved cache performance for folder notes.

### ✨ Features

>3. You will see an old folder named `obsidian-portals`. Inside it, find the file `data.json` – this contains all your portal configurations.

>5. **Copy the `data.json` file** from the `obsidian-portals` folder into the new `portals` folder.
>6. (Optional) After confirming everything works, you may delete the old `obsidian-portals` folder.

releases.description:
# Version 1.1.2 - Bookmark groups, Collapsible Floating Buttons, UI Polish and Bug Fixes

### ⚠️ Important: Manual migration required if you had any previous version (v1.1.0 or earlier)
>The plugin ID has changed from `obsidian-portals` to `portals` to follow Obsidian's guidelines. Your settings won't be updated automatically for this round. If you are updating from an older version (1.1.0 or earlier), please follow these steps to keep your settings:
>1. **Close Obsidian** completely.
>2. **Navigate to your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/` folder**.
>3. You will see an old folder named `obsidian-portals`. Inside it, find the file `data.json` – this contains all your portal configurations.
>4. **Create a new folder** named `portals` in the same location (if it doesn't already exist).
>5. **Copy the `data.json` file** from the `obsidian-portals` folder into the new `portals` folder.
>6. (Optional) After confirming everything works, you may delete the old `obsidian-portals` folder.
>7. **Restart Obsidian** and enable the new plugin (`Portals`). Your settings should now be restored.
>8. If you prefer, you can start fresh – your old settings will not be used automatically.
## v1.1.1 – Minor Updates, Manifest Change. 

Portals lets you pin any folder or tag as a customisable tab – a Portal into your selected folder trees. 
### 🆕 Updates
- Support for bookmark groups. Create bookmark groups in obsidian bookmarks. Once created you can access them through Side Portal bookmarks, add/ remove items on them. Any edits into bookmarks through Obsidian or Portals syncs across either bookmark tabs. 
- Floating buttons for new file, new folder, sort and collapse are now foldable. Toggle fold and unfold using Right Click on Collapse button or Long Press on Mobile. 
- UI improvements on fonts, tooltips, borders, spacing, consistent feature behaviour between Portal tabs and Side Portal tabs. 
- Improved hover tooltips. Now hover tool tips only show on inactive tabs if the Show Inactive Tab Names setting is off. 
- Fixed an issue regarding the visual feedback on floating buttons on mobile. 
- Fixed an issue regarding Tab Buttons not showing color despite setting toggle on. 
- Performance, data write & memory updates for a snappier experience. 
- Bug Fixes and improvements. 

Note: Side Portal is a new feature under testing. For best results,
- On mobile: Works best with Baseline Theme right now as the theme renders vault on the top side. On other themes, turn off floating navigation in settings, i.e. Settings > Appearance > Floating Navigation. 
- On desktop, you may need to turn off vault hiding if your theme hides the vault on the lower side. 
### ✨ Features

- Pin any folder or tag to the top of the file pane as a Tab.
- Side Portal - a collapsible, resizable split view for recent tabs, bookmarks and folder notes
- Folder Notes - A dedicated note that can be used for folder context, accessed by Cmd/Ctrl+Click on folder or directly as a file.
- Recent File list - A single place to view recently accessed files, based on last opening in editor, from across the vault. 
- Bookmarks - Synced to obsidian bookmarks, supports files & urls. Groups created from obsidian bookmarks can be used and edited. 
- Custom icons for Tabs (Phosphor set) and option to add tab colours with opacity/gradient options.
- Full file tree with drag‑and‑drop (desktop only) and folder‑only sorting. Drag and Drop is not available between portal spaces. 
- Complete right‑click context menus (same as default explorer).
- Floating, foldable action buttons: new note, new folder, collapse all, sort. Right Click on collapse / Long Press to fold/unfold.
- Native sorting (name, created, modified, asc/desc).
- Mobile‑friendly design (Android Tested)
- Export/import settings using JSon File. Recommended to save on json file after your primary settings. 

### ⚙️ Installation

#### Using BRAT (Beta Reviewers Auto-update Tester)

- Install BRAT from Community Plugins
- Open Settings, **Add Beta Plugin**
- Enter Repository URL: [https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals)
- Click **Add Plugin**, BRAT will download and enable the latest release.

#### Manual download

- Download release package.
- Copy the files named `main.js`, `styles.css`, `manifest.json` and place in `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-portals/`

### 📝 Notes

- This is a pre‑release – please test and report any issues!
- See the [[README](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme)](https://github.com/samaraliwarsi/obsidian-portals#readme) for full details and screenshots.
